DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE NOV-14-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS JOSE CALLES, AND JOSE CASTILLO PEREZ'S MOTION COMPELLING COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT'S AUGUST 15, 2024, DISCOVERY ORDER; AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: IF RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS HAVE NOT BEEN PRODUCED BEFORE THE HEARING, THEN THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED AND A COMPLETE PRODUCTION DUE WITHIN 10 COURT DAYS UNLESS OTHERWISE AGREED. THE REQUEST FOR DAILY ACCRUING SANCTIONS IS DENIED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND EMAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM SCOTT BORROWMAN. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (525/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Mar-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFFS JOSE CALLES, AN INDIVIDUAL, and JOSE CASTILLO PEREZ' Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Ryan Ardi, Esq., of Quill & Arrow, LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiffs.
Alexandria Pappas, Esq., of Park Lawless & Tremonti LLP, (213) 640-3770, for Defendant.

The matter is discussed. At the conclusion of discussion, Plaintiff's motion to compel the deposition of the defendant's person most knowledegable and production of documents is granted. Dates RE production of persons most knowledgeable are discussed in open court and shall be determined by the pending motion to continue the trial date of May-12-2025.

Plaintiff's counsel is to submit a proposed order to the court via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org which conforms to the dates RE production of persons most knowledgeable as discussed and confirmed in open court, and in conformance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion Compelling Compliance With The Court'S August 15, 2024, Discovery Order; And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-14-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. ROY ENAV, Esq. of Quill & Arrow, LLP (tel: 310-933-4271) for Plaintiffs.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS JOSE CALLES,AND JOSE CASTILLO PEREZ's Motion Compelling Compliance With The Court'S August 15, 2024, Discovery Order; And Request For Sanctions.

Matter not argued/contested. Counsel Roy Enav states responsive documents have not been produced and inquires the court as to the content of a proposed order, as the court issued two tentative rulings. The Court instructs counsel to prepare a proposed order that reflects the tentative ruling issued in case documents were not produced prior to the hearing. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

If responsive documents have not been produced before the hearing, then the motion will be granted and a complete production due within 10 court days unless otherwise agreed. The request for daily accruing sanctions is DENIED. The Code does not permit courts to impose such accruing sanctions. Rather, CCP 2023.010 and 2031.310 (the sections cited in the motion) require sanctions be tailored to the reasonable expenses, including fees, incurred as a result of the misconduct, but Plaintiffs did not identify any such basis to award sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and email it to Judge Pro Tem Scott Borrowman.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)
